subject: soccer moms
one_liner: The AI-powered IDE for family operations. Write the carpool in YAML, tab-complete the snack rotation, and let an agent argue with the other parents for you.
brand_voice_notes: Dev-tool gravitas applied without irony to youth soccer logistics. Everything is phrased like editor documentation: completions, diagnostics, merge conflicts, agents. The family is a codebase and nobody at the company finds this strange. Never wink. The absurdity lives in the specificity (guilt as a model signal, Rockford away games, rice krispies declared twice in week 6).

hero.h1: Your family is a codebase. Start maintaining it.
hero.sub: Sideline is an AI pair-programmer for the school year. It reads your season, predicts the carpool, and proposes the snack rotation as a diff. You just press Tab.

features.title: Tab to accept the carpool
features.blurb: Sideline predicts who drives Saturday from 40 signals, including proximity, minivan capacity, and guilt. Press Tab to accept. There is no undo, which we consider a commitment device.

features.title: snacks.toml
features.blurb: The snack rotation is config now. Versioned, reviewable, enforced. When two mothers declare rice krispies in week 6, Sideline raises a merge conflict instead of letting it happen at the field.

features.title: Agent Mode
features.blurb: An autonomous agent messages the group chat on your behalf until someone volunteers, escalating politely every 45 minutes. Marked safe after extensive testing on one family.

pricing_tiers.name: Orange Slices
perks: Read-only access to the group chat

pricing_tiers.name: Team Parent
pricing_tiers.tagline: The tier you were voluntold into.
perks: Agent replies in your tone, but nicer

pricing_tiers.name: Travel Team
pricing_tiers.tagline: Priced like the actual travel team.
perks: A dedicated agent that talks to the other agents
Priority lane at the concession stand (pilot)

testimonials.quote: I pressed Tab and it assigned my ex-husband the away game in Rockford. The model knew.
testimonials.name: Dana Whitfield
testimonials.title: Snack Coordinator (Interim), Cedar Ridge Strikers U-9

testimonials.quote: We migrated the whole family to Sideline in March. The kids are in the config now. I haven't opened a group chat since.
testimonials.name: Priya Raghavan
testimonials.title: Carpool Lead & Release Manager, Maple Glen Fusion

testimonials.quote: Agent Mode negotiated goal-side photo duty with another family's agent. Neither of us was consulted. The photos were fine.
testimonials.name: Beth Okafor
testimonials.title: Director of Sideline Operations, one (1) minivan

demo_screen_concept: A dark IDE window on the family-ops repo. Left: file tree (carpool.yaml, snacks.toml, saturday.md, brayden.json). Center: syntax-highlighted carpool.yaml open in a tabbed editor, with ghost-text AI completion suggesting the week-7 driver assignment; pressing Tab actually accepts it and the next suggestion appears. Bottom: a Problems panel with 2 diagnostics (Maddox has no ride home, line 14; rice_krispies declared twice in week 6) that jump-highlight the offending line when clicked. Right: an Agent pane where the Sideline agent proposes messaging Jennifer's mom about Saturday and cycles through canned escalations when you click 'run agent'. File tabs switch between the four files.

images.rationale: hero skipped because the design direction is a typographic dark-IDE composition (self-typing editor window, not a photo); demo assets skipped because the demo is a code editor (document-shaped, no photo slots); full budget on 3 testimonial headshots, the landing page's named users

items.prompt: Deadpan corporate editorial headshot photograph of a tired but composed suburban mother in her mid-40s, light brown hair in a practical ponytail, wearing a maroon athletic quarter-zip pullover, faint polite smile that does not reach her eyes, head-and-shoulders framing, one focal subject, studio lighting with a soft indigo-blue rim light hinting at #7B8CF0, plain deep graphite charcoal studio backdrop near #14161C, muted moody dark-mode palette, crisp professional corporate-directory portrait. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

design_direction.style: Dark-mode IDE, syntax-theme editor mode. The whole page is rendered as a code editor after sunset: a deep graphite editor ground with a faint line-number gutter running down the hero margin, panels drawn as editor windows with slim tab bars and hairline #2A2E3A borders, and a status bar for a footer. One syntax-indigo carries the brand, keywords, links, headings' underlines, and structure; one rationed orange-slice orange is reserved strictly for the single primary CTA, the Tab-key affordance, and the live agent status dot, and appears almost nowhere else. Space Grotesk sets the wordmark and headlines in confident grotesque; Inter carries calm body copy. Everything that pretends to be code or telemetry (YAML snippets, diagnostics counts, tier prices, completion stats) is set in JetBrains Mono with tabular figures so it reads like an editor buffer, not a marketing number. Syntax-highlighted YAML uses the palette only: indigo keys, sage-green strings, muted comments. Ghost-text completions render at 45% opacity with a blinking block cursor. Motion is editor-native: the hero types its own carpool.yaml then snaps the ghost text solid on a simulated Tab press, the command palette fades and scales in at 150ms, diagnostics tick in with a small stagger. No gradients beyond a faint vignette on the editor ground, no glassmorphism, no neon glow soup, no light mode. Reads like a venture-funded developer-tools company that has been pointed, with total seriousness, at a woman who needs Jennifer's mom to answer before Thursday.
design_direction.archetype: Developer-tool SaaS landing (dark, Linear-adjacent): split hero with live self-typing editor window, feature rows as editor panels, pricing as plan cards in a tab bar, status-bar footer.
design_direction.motion_vocab: blinking block cursor, ghost-text typing then Tab-accept snap, command-palette fade/scale at 150ms, staggered diagnostic ticks, soft pulse on the agent status dot
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Left column: h1, sub, orange CTA plus a fake keyboard 'Tab' keycap hint. Right column: an editor window titled 'family-ops — carpool.yaml' with traffic-light dots, line numbers, syntax-highlighted YAML that types itself, ghost completion, and a one-line status bar (main*, 2 problems, Agent: idle).
